creator: rkoberman@gmail.com
text:
This is an issue with the 'pkg version' command.It does not yet understand the OSVERSION based versions flavors recently introduced to allow packages to be built whenever the OSVERSION is updated, This shoid solve the problem of the need to build the ports anytime a kernel is changed significantly. Unfortunately, at this time it is not understood and, every time the OSVERSION is updated, this will show up as an out of date version until your system is updated to match the most recent OSVERSION.

As long as you are running 1500026, you are fine. When/if you update to 15000329, you are up to date.

'pkg upgrade' does the right thing and will only build the proper version for any system. It's just the 'pkg version' is confused until it is fixed. This is a real bug, but olny causes confusion, not any real problems.
